text_content: P1063940 Tag A 32-bit identifier for a data element, represented as a pair of four-digit hexadecimal numbers, the "group" and the "element". If the "group" number is odd, the tag is for a private (manufacturer-specific) data element. Examples: (0010,0020) [Patient ID], (07FE,0010) [Pixel Data], (0019,0210) [private data element] Transfer Syntax The encoding used for exchange of DICOM information objects and messages. Examples: JPEG compressed (images), little endian explicit value representation. Unique Identifier (UID) A globally unique "dotted decimal" string that identifies a specific object or a class of objects; an ISO-8824 Object Identifier. Examples: Study Instance UID, SOP Class UID, SOP Instance UID. Value Representation (VR) The format type of an individual DICOM data element, such as text, an integer, a person's name, or a code. DICOM information objects can be transmitted with either explicit identification of the type of each data element

text_content: Provided input to traceability evaluation based upon requirements from both domestic and international regulatory authorities and approved this Plan. 根据国内和国际监管机构的要求，为可追溯性评估提供输入，并批准本计划。 4. Upgrades and Conversions: Upgrades from a previous version to HAL 4.0 can be made in some countries where HAL 4.0 is cleared. The details of which countries allow this will be detailed later in the project. For China, the system updates cannot engage any re-labelling activities, the information on device label should not be changed, such as manufacturing date, model name, NMPA license number etc. . Conversions from Halcyon to Ethos radiotherapy sytem can be made in some countries. Conversions involve changes to the covers, installing and enabling the Ethos TPS (On-couch session manager or OSM) function and changing the nameplate to include “Ethos Radiotherapy System” while manufacturing date and serial number remain the same. The UDI will be updated to describe the changed model. Regulatory Plan Revision Doc Number
